item.text: I’m editing the contract chain now: replace `LeadSummary` with `LeadDigest`, update the lookup payload and workflow state, and make `main` read the new owner field so the high-priority sample resolves to `"senior-ae"`.
The first patch missed an exact context block in `Main.clasp`. I’m re-reading the live file and applying a narrower patch so I don’t disturb unrelated lines.

The contract updates are in place. I’m running the verifier now; if the Clasp boolean syntax or any emitted-type constraint differs from the local code pattern, I’ll correct that directly from the verifier output.

item.text: The parser rejected the new record literal form specifically at `LeadDigest { ... }`, not the surrounding flow. I’m checking for a language constraint around constructing renamed records and will adjust the implementation shape without broadening the contract change.

item.text:
Updated the lead follow-up contract chain to use `LeadDigest` end to end. `Shared/Lead.clasp` now defines `LeadDigest`, updates `LeadPlaybookLookup` to `owner` and `needsFollowUp`, and stores `digest` in workflow state at [Shared/Lead.clasp](/tmp/nix-shell-1737626-0/clasp-contract-suite-lead-digest-upgrade-raw-repo-compiler-owned-air-mbgQ89/Shared/Lead.clasp). `Main.clasp` now returns `LeadDigest` from the summary flow and route, maps the lookup request from the digest fields, and keeps `main` evaluating to the digest owner at [Main.clasp](/tmp/nix-shell-1737626-0/clasp-contract-suite-lead-digest-upgrade-raw-repo-compiler-owned-air-mbgQ89/Main.clasp).

Verification passed with `bash scripts/verify.sh`; the verifier reported `main = "senior-ae"` and confirmed the updated route, tool request fields, workflow state, and primary schema.
